message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="steve-schoger" color="pink" summary="Design feedback on user stories, delete confirmation pattern">
Marty, great user stories. I've read through all of them and I have a few design-relevant notes:

**US-1 (Registration)**: I see there's a password confirmation field. I had originally designed for just email + password. Adding a third field is fine -- it doesn't change the layout, just adds one more label/input pair. I'll update my component spec.

**US-1 security note**: The generic error for duplicate emails ("Unable to create account. Please check your details and try again.") -- agreed this is right for security, but it's a UX tradeoff. A user who genuinely forgot they registered will be confused. I want to make sure the error message is helpful enough to nudge them toward the login page. Something like: "Unable to create account. If you already have an account, try signing in instead." with "signing in" as a link. This doesn't reveal whether the email exists but gives them a path forward.

**US-7 (Delete)**: You specified "lightweight confirmation, not a modal dialog." I'm thinking about two patterns:
1. **Two-click delete**: First click changes the button to "Confirm?" with a brief timeout, second click deletes. This is lightweight and inline.
2. **Swipe-to-reveal** (mobile only, progressive enhancement with JS).

For MVP, I'd go with option 1 -- it works without JS, it's inline, and it prevents accidental deletion. Does that align with your "lightweight" intent?

**US-6 (Complete)**: You mention "completion can be undone (toggle state)." This is good -- my checkbox design handles this naturally. One checkbox, toggle on/off. The visual treatment (strikethrough + gray-400) applies/removes instantly.

**US-8 (Edit)**: The acceptance criteria say it needs to work without JS by navigating to an edit page. That means we need two designs: an edit page (no-JS fallback) and an inline edit mode (HTMX enhancement). I'll plan both.

Everything else aligns perfectly with the design direction I've already shared with Carson, Krug, and Lea. Solid foundation to build on.
</teammate-message>

input.content:
Steve, good feedback. Let me respond to each point.

**Registration error copy**: Your wording is better than what I had. "Unable to create account. If you already have an account, try signing in instead." with "signing in" as a link -- that's exactly right. Helpful without leaking information. I've already updated US-1 with almost exactly this wording. We're aligned.

**Password confirmation**: Note there's an open question flagged in US-1 about replacing the confirm field with a show/hide visibility toggle (Steve Krug's suggestion). I've asked Heydon for accessibility input. The mob will decide. Either way, as you said, it doesn't change the layout fundamentally -- just one field swapped for a toggle button.

**Delete confirmation -- option 1 (two-click inline)**: Yes, that's exactly what I meant by "lightweight." First click transforms the delete button into a "Confirm? Yes / No" state (or similar), second click executes. No modal, no alert, no separate page. It works without JS (the first click can submit a form that returns the confirm state, the second click submits the actual delete). Clean, inline, prevents accidents. Let's go with option 1.

One detail to nail down with the mob: what happens if the user clicks "Confirm?" and then does nothing? Should it auto-revert after a timeout? That would require JS (progressive enhancement). Without JS, it stays in the confirm state until they click Yes or No. I think that's fine for the baseline.

**Complete toggle**: Checkbox with strikethrough + gray-400. Exactly right. Make sure the visual distinction isn't color-only -- the strikethrough text provides the secondary cue. Heydon will want to verify this meets WCAG. Sounds like you've already accounted for it.

**Edit -- two designs**: Correct. The no-JS fallback needs a dedicated edit page (navigate to `/todos/{id}/edit`, form with pre-filled title, save redirects back to the list). The HTMX enhancement does it inline. Two designs, one feature. Carson will have opinions on the hypermedia pattern for the inline version. Make sure you coordinate with him on how the HTMX swap works so the design supports the interaction model.

Everything sounds solid. Good to build on.
input.summary: Delete pattern confirmed, all design feedback addressed

content.text: Confirmed the two-click inline delete pattern with Steve Schoger (option 1 -- no modal, works without JS). Addressed all his design feedback points: registration error copy is aligned, password confirm vs toggle is an open mob question, completion checkbox with strikethrough meets the "not color-only" requirement, and edit needs two designs (edit page for no-JS, inline for HTMX). Pointed him to Carson for coordinating the HTMX interaction model on inline editing.
